exemplar.title: Kiffe Kiffe Demain par Faiza Guene.

detailedExemplarData.title: Kiffe Kiffe Demain par Faiza Guene.

strengths: Broad and appropriate lexical range, including idiomatic expressions
Effective use of both simple and complex grammatical structures
Generally accurate language with only occasional minor errors
Clear demonstration of prosodic awareness through idiomatic contractions
weaknesses: A few spelling and agreement lapses (e.g. âPourguoiâ, missing agreements)
Occasional register slips between sociolect and standard French
Minor inconsistencies in complex structure accuracy
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work firmly in the 7â9 band for vocabulary (8), grammar (7), accuracy (7) and pronunciation (8). The student shows convincing variety and purposeful idiomatic usage, along with effective manipulation of complex syntax. Occasional lapses prevent placement at the very top of the scale, but overall performance merits a high 9 out of 12.

strengths: Consistently relevant and persuasive engagement with the literary extract
Effective exploitation of quotations to launch well-structured reflections
Well-developed and supported observations linking text themes to contemporary examples
weaknesses: Extract is reproduced in full without accompanying close-reading commentary
Lacks focused analysis on how respect is portrayed and its wider implications
Could enrich interpretation by contextualizing edition or translation choices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are assessed at 5 in the top band (5â6). The plan is coherent, thematically anchored and convincingly developed, justifying full marks (6/6) for consistently relevant and effective use of the extract, despite some missing analytic depth.

strengths: Questions and prompts are consistently tailored to extract themes, showing clear relevance
Planned responses indicate breadth of scope and personal stance with evaluation
Use of examples and occasional humor suggests depth of engagement
weaknesses: No actual student responses provided to demonstrate real-time relevance or depth
Multiple sections lack personal examples or structured commentary
Absence of modeled answers prevents full assessment of conversational fluency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place conversation planning in the top band (5â6) with a mid-band score of 5. While questions and prompts are thoughtfully constructed, the lack of any student-generated responses and personal examples limits the award to 5 out of 6.

strengths: Logical sequencing of follow-up questions implies sustained comprehension
Evidence of readiness for independent contributions and rhetorical engagement
Prompts show capability to maintain conversational thread without repetition
weaknesses: Limited concrete evidence of real-time comprehension or spontaneous repair
Some suggested intercultural and personal reflections are missing
Not fully effortless; small lapses expected
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Interactive skills are assessed at band 5 in comprehension and participation. The plan suggests sustained interaction and independent input, but minor lapses and the hypothetical nature of the material prevent a perfect score. Awarding 5 out of 6 reflects strong but not seamless communication ability.

strandSelections.reasoning: Across the extract analysis and the anticipated conversation, the student demonstrates confident control of both simple and complex syntax: relative clauses (Â« â¦quâon a choisis Â»), conditional constructions (Â« Si tu pouvais ajouterâ¦ Â»), passive voice (Â« â¦est-il en train dâÃªtre tuÃ© Â») and reported speech.  The range is not entirely flawlessâthere are missing agreements (Â« lâaime pas Â», Â« vieux Ã  qui Â») and the register occasionally slipsâbut variety is clearly beyond the âbasic-plusâ level and largely effective, aligning with band 7-9.

Even though only written material is available, orthography and accent choice suggest a speaker capable of clear, natural pronunciation (e.g. correct elisions, maintenance of register).  Idiomatic contractions replicate authentic banlieue intonation, indicating an ability to use prosody to support meaning.  This supports a high 7â9 placement.

The dossier reveals readiness to offer independent comments, segue into related ideas, and pose rhetorical questionsâevidence of proactive participation.  Consistent, independent contribution corresponds to the 5-6 band; awarding 5 recognises quality while reserving full marks for a wholly effortless performance.

The studentâs outline shows every planned question anchored in the thematic concerns of the extractârespect at school, family dynamics, absence of aspirationâmaking the presentation consistently relevant.  The line of inquiry is coherent and persuasive, warranting the top band (5-6) with a mid-band score.

Planned interventions show personal stance, examples and occasional humour, giving breadth and depth (e.g. discussing break-dance as discipline olympique, gender pay gap).  This engagement meets descriptors for âbroad in scope and depthâ, meriting mid-top band 5.

Sample answers and prompts indicate that the student can tailor replies directly to each question, moving beyond surface agreement toward evaluation (e.g. weighing âamisâ vs âfamilleâ, condemning âtourisme de masseâ).  Relevance is constant and responses are well-shaped, justifying 5 in the 5-6 band.

The range and logical sequencing of follow-up questions imply that, in real time, the student would grasp nuances quickly and maintain the conversational thread without needing repetition.  Such sustained comprehension aligns with the highest band, though small lapses expected in spontaneous speech justify a 5 rather than 6.

Specific quotations (ââ¦tout Ã§a pour se donner bonne conscienceâ¦â) are exploited to launch well-structured reflections on hypocrisy, social justice and education policy.  Observations are expanded through concrete contemporary examples (volontourisme, distanciel/presentiel) showing sustained development; hence a solid 5 within the effective band.

The student manipulates a broad lexical field, drawing on both colloquial and more formal registers (e.g. Â« prophÃ¨te social Â», Â« volontourisme Â», Â« empreinte carbone Â») that suit the satirical tone of the extract and the wide-ranging nature of the follow-up questions.  Idiomatic expressions such as Â« se la joue Â», Â« jâen ai marre Â» and Â« faire face au monde du travail Â» appear naturally and enhance meaning.  Although a handful of items (âPourguoiâ, âlâaime pasâ) reveal lapses or intentional representation of sociolect, overall variety and appropriateness are convincing rather than merely adequate, meriting placement in the 7â9 band rather than the 4â6 band.

Minor spelling or agreement errors occur (e.g. Â« facon Â», Â« Pourguoi Â»), yet meaning is always transparent and cohesion remains intact.  Complex sentences are generally accurate, allowing the message to flow without breakdown.  Such accuracy, with only occasional slips, fits comfortably within the 7â9 descriptors.

annotations.comment: The student correctly identifies and cites the source of the extract, demonstrating awareness of proper bibliographic presentation.
The extract is reproduced in full but lacks any accompanying analysis or interpretation. Add focused commentary on how respect is portrayed and its social implications.
While the publication details (Livre de Poche) are included, the student should contextualize the editionâs significance or translation choices to enrich analysis.
The section header signals the start of conversation questions, but no student responses are provided. Include sample answers to demonstrate engagement.
The âRelations socialesâ theme is well organized but lacks student-generated ideas or personal examples that would deepen the conversation.
The first friendship question introduces a core conversational theme, but without any student response its relevance cannot be assessed or developed.
The question âLes amis sont-ils plus importants que la famille?â is thought-provoking; the student should model a balanced answer with examples.
The question on preparing for future work is forward-looking; include a response detailing skills or experiences to demonstrate depth.
The ethical question on child labor invites nuanced argumentation; the student has not provided any justification or stance.
The question on the relevance and politicization of the Olympics contextually supports contemporary debate; include critical response and examples.
The âVacances et voyagesâ section is clearly marked but lacks travel reflections or anecdotes; student should include personal narratives.
The term âvolontourismeâ is introduced, showing lexical range; student should define and critique it with concrete examples.
The âÃducationâ section is clearly signposted but contains no student observations. Provide structured commentary to show critical engagement with schooling themes.
The question about compulsory schooling invites policy reflection; the student should offer stance and justify with country-specific data.
The final question on parental education differences is promising for personal insight but lacks any student reflection or examples.
The initial tweet of questions on page 3 shows range, yet the absence of student stance prevents assessment of relevance or depth.
The prompt âQuel est ton cours prÃ©fÃ©rÃ©â¦â is effective for eliciting personal detail; the student should include a clear, analytical response.
Though the âActivitÃ©s de loisirsâ header organizes the theme well, student elaboration on personal hobbies is missing.
The sports values question is well formulated; student should articulate specific attributes and link to personal or cultural examples.
The environmental impact question on tourism is timely but lacks any student argument or data to substantiate a position.
The opening question on historical sites raises a critical issue; student should analyze case studies or local examples to develop depth.
The question on doping in high-level sports opens an ethical debate; the student should present a reasoned stance supported by research.
The carbon-footprint question is highly relevant but would benefit from the student outlining concrete personal actions or policy proposals.
The cultural question on must-visit sites invites descriptive and evaluative language; the student should include personal recommendations and rationale.
The query on inappropriate tourist behavior could illustrate intercultural awareness; include examples and personal reflections to strengthen participation.
